The '''''Delta''-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport''' was an assault vehicle, built by [[Telgorn Corporation/Legends|Telgorn Corporation]], which was designed to carry [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] into battle.

==Characteristics==
[[File:Old_Stormtrooper_Transport.jpg|thumb|left|160px|An older design of the DX-9, also found throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]]]
The ''Delta''-class DX-9 was armed with eight [[KT4 laser cannon|KT4]] [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], four [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and two projectile launchers that were usually loaded with [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]]. These weapons allowed the DX-9 to disable and board targets with light to moderate defenses. Very strong [[Deflector shield/Legends|shielding]], particularly forward of the vessel, allowed the transport to weather considerable resistance from its targets. A universal docking mechanism allowed the DX-9 to attach itself to most ships, and, if necessary, the transport could also bore its own hole into the enemy hull.<ref name="SC" />

The DX-9 only had weapons on its forward arc, and was not very maneuverable, so it was vulnerable to [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] attacks. These [[Transport/Legends|transports]] were usually deployed with fighter escorts or as part of a larger assault force.<ref name="TF">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>

==History==
The stormtrooper transport saw a great deal of action during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], not only for boarding operations and deploying troops into battle, but also as a personnel transport for use between [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. It also could be used to resupply [[Bomber/Legends|bombers]] and repair other craft.<ref name="TF" /> ''[[Omega]]'' and ''[[Sigma]]'' were notable Imperial teams of DX-9s that participated in many missions.

Although designed for the Galactic Empire, many DX-9s were also often used by other factions, most notably the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

The Empire eventually developed a successor to the ''Delta''-class, the [[Gamma-class ATR-6 assault transport|''Gamma''-class ATR-6 assault transport]]. The larger ATR-6 lacked frontal laser cannons, instead having laser turrets to cover firing arcs that were blind spots on the DX-9. The Imperial Navy continued to use the ''Delta''-class for most assault and boarding operations, turning to the ''Gamma''-class if especially heavy opposition was expected. A further development was the [[Beta-class ETR-3 escort transport|''Beta''-class ETR-3 escort transport]], which combined the DX-9's frontal weaponry with the ATR-6's turrets.<ref name="XWA" />

In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], one of these transports was deployed by the forces of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Devlia]], based on [[Vladet]], against [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron's]] base on [[Talasea]]. Following the failure of that surprise covert assault, the transport was captured by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces.<ref name="XRS">''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''</ref> In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] during the [[Battle of Altyr V (Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Altyr V]], DX-9s deployed stormtroopers against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] base on [[Altyr V]].<ref name="MS">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Mysteries of the Sith]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
In [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']], the DX-9 is known as simply the ''Transport'' or ''TRN''. However, official strategy guides have referred to it as the ''Stormtrooper Transport'' to avoid confusion with other craft with similar functions, and it adopted the latter name for later titles in the series.

In ''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'' (as well as ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''), the [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] symbols "NGC" are found on the backside of the ship.

An unidentified ship greatly resembling the DX-9 transport appeared in the [[Star Wars Annual (2015) 1|''Star Wars Annual'' (2015) 1]], a [[canon]] one-shot comic published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[December 9]], [[2015]].<ref name="Annual 1">[[Star Wars Annual (2015) 1|''Star Wars Annual'' (2015) 1]]</ref>
